title: Any centos container sticks at login command if run any command before
body:
### Issue Description

If run any command before login command at docker container. It sticks forever.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```bash
podman run --rm -it centos:7 sh -c 'true; login'
```

### Describe the results you received

container doesn't responds after login command.

### Describe the results you expected

container responds and ask user name.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

ubuntu

### Additional information

I have the same behaviour in docker and on macOS version of docker. On another version of centos the result is the same. On ubuntu containers everything is ok.

`strace` show that someone want's to close all FD starting from `0xFFFFF` :D

```bash
strace -f podman run --rm -it centos bash -c 'true; login' &> kek
```

```
[pid 2087467] close(1048575)            = -1 EBADF (Bad file descriptor)
[pid 2087467] close(1048574)            = -1 EBADF (Bad file descriptor)
[pid 2087467] close(1048573)            = -1 EBADF (Bad file descriptor)
[pid 2087467] close(1048572)            = -1 EBADF (Bad file descriptor)
[pid 2087467] close(1048571)            = -1 EBADF (Bad file descriptor)
[pid 2087467] close(1048570)            = -1 EBADF (Bad file descriptor)
[pid 2087467] close(1048569)            = -1 EBADF (Bad file descriptor)
...
```

comments.body: This is not a podman bug, I you think this is a bug with the centos image/login command you should report this against centos and not Podman.
